behaviors.description: Command segmentation treats newline-separated statements as separate segments, not one monolithic block.
behaviors.justification: Seam-map: local function seam (command segmentation/parsing logic). MOCK-MISS: the stated failure can be triggered directly by parser input/output without requiring module handoff. REAL-INFRA/LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection-gate: rejected Integration because behavior text is only about segmentation correctness, not downstream module interaction.
behaviors.test_description: Call the segmentation function with a multiline shell snippet containing newline-separated assignments and a command; assert it returns multiple ordered segments rather than one combined segment.

behaviors.description: A multi-line shell block with variable assignments followed by `gh pr create` is recognized as a PR-create trigger.
behaviors.justification: Seam-map: module/interface seam between segmentation and trigger classification. MOCK-MISS: the incident came from whole-block classification as `not_a_trigger`, which appears when parsed segments are handed to trigger logic. REAL-INFRA: no real network/subprocess required for detection itself. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection-gate: rejected System because behavior text is about recognizing command text pattern, not executing `gh` or handling OS/network effects.
behaviors.test_description: Run the hook’s detection pipeline end-to-end in-process (segmenter + trigger matcher) on a multiline block where assignments precede `gh pr create`; assert final classification is PR-create trigger.

behaviors.description: A similar multi-line block that ends with `gh issue create` is not incorrectly treated as a PR-create trigger.
behaviors.justification: Seam-map: module/interface seam (segmentation + trigger discrimination rules). MOCK-MISS: false-positive risk appears in combined parsing/classification flow, not just isolated token matching. REAL-INFRA/LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection-gate: rejected System because no behavior text requires real subprocess/API execution; only classification outcome is under test.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the same in-process detection pipeline on multiline input ending with `gh issue create`; assert classification is not PR-create trigger (e.g., `not_a_trigger` or issue-trigger class).

behaviors.description: When a PR-create trigger is detected, the hook can reconstruct and persist the PR identifier needed by the review loop state.
behaviors.justification: Seam-map: module/interface seam plus local persistence seam (identifier extraction + state writer). MOCK-MISS: pure unit stubs can hide serialization/field-mapping mismatches across extractor and writer. REAL-INFRA: local filesystem is sufficient; no OS/network/subprocess dependency is required. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection-gate: rejected System because behavior text requires persistence, not real external command/API behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Feed a detected PR-create event payload through identifier reconstruction and state persistence modules using a temp local filesystem path; assert the persisted state contains the expected PR identifier fields/values.

behaviors.description: After PR-create detection, the review gate state is written in a form that the pre-tool enforcement hook can read and enforce.
behaviors.justification: Seam-map: module/interface seam across producer/consumer contract (PostToolUse writer -> PreToolUse reader/enforcer) via local state file. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no required real OS/network/subprocess behavior beyond local file read/write, (b) no AI/ML output dependence, (c) no multi-agentic chain. Rejection-gate: rejected System because behavior text focuses on schema/contract compatibility for state handoff, which can be validated fully in-process with local filesystem.
behaviors.test_description: In one test, run PostToolUse state-writing path after PR-create detection, then invoke PreToolUse enforcement reader against that written state; assert enforcement logic detects active review gate and applies the expected block/allow decision.
